content:
@Alexis Hazell: we wanted to have a simple web page somewhere for this community, where we could host a calendar and a code of conduct, and some other basic information, etc.
I see that you're the assistant system administrator for the nLab — do you think there's any chance this page might be hosted there?
I host websites if there is a problem with getting a site. Maybe we could have a WordPress site for public dissemination of basic CT.
@Daniel Geisler: yes, I think the main concern is finding somewhere we can reliably host a page or two
now that I think about it, we could do something as simple as creating a GitHub account and hosting it on GitHub pages or something
we just thought it would be nice if it was somewhere already known for category theory
Do we own a domain?
no

categorytheory.com for around $100
categorytheory.world $2
where do you see the first one?
that's cheaper than I would have expected, though I'm not sure who would be happy to cover that cost
Godaddy
it's not being displayed as available to me
It is owned but for sale. $69 plus commission.
ah, I see
I just bid on categorytheory.com. We can now consider where money might come from and who should own the domain.
i think @Jelle Herold owns applied.cat
For sure we have some category-theory related domains
OMG are we a Petri net?
Well, we Statebox for sure are, in a way or another
I guess UCR is too

think @Jelle Herold owns applied.cat

I thought the .cat TLD was explicitly only for websites promoting the Catalan language/culture, so this would be disallowed
I don't know if others self-identify as esteemed members of the Petri net bandwagon, but I hope they do!

Nathanael Arkor said:

think @Jelle Herold owns applied.cat

I thought the .cat TLD was explicitly only for websites promoting the Catalan language/culture, so this would be disallowed

We know, you have to put at least one sentence in catalan somewhere and you are good to go.
that seems disingenuous, but okay :upside_down:
Lol, it's for sure not optimal, but on the other hand there is nothing better than a .cat domain for a category theory website, I guess :D
it is a nice one
But we also have another website about applied category theory where we started doing things... This was born after a NIST workshop in 2018 but then it went nowhere

The idea was having a repository where everyone could register and share information such as what they worked on, where they were based etc, but as it often happens then everyone involved was too busy doing something else and the whole thing was forgotten T_T
theoryofcategories.com is available cheaply
it's far too addictive looking for domain names…
Don't tell me xD I try to stay away from these things, it's very easy to end up with a 1000€ yearly bill only for all the cool looking domains one wants to buy :D
categorytheory.education and categorytheory.edu are also available
(I didn't look at the price though)
.edu is reserved for US academic institutions, I believe?
Once owned domains only cost $20 a year to be renewed.

Hey all, I hope this is the right place to post this.

I'd be interested in contributing monthly donations to any hosting costs related to this site if someone set up a Patreon for the community.
Is there some reason not to use nLab or Azimuth? One of those sounds like the obvious thing by far
@Jules Hedges: someone could reach out to Richard Williamson, who is the administrator

Nathanael Arkor said:

think @Jelle Herold owns applied.cat

I thought the .cat TLD was explicitly only for websites promoting the Catalan language/culture, so this would be disallowed

Hah I don't think this enforced in any way, shape or form.
@John Baez We're discussing hosting a webpage for the calendar, guide, code of conduct etc. Do you think nLab, Azimuth, or a separate website would be best?
Azimuth Forum you mean?
Or Azimuth Wiki? Or Azimuth Blog?
The Azimuth Wiki has the great advantage that anyone can edit it now and put information there.
I'd be perfectly happy with that use of it.
Yes, and that seems to be the only one of the three that's amenable to a permanent page.
The nLab would work similarly, but you'd want to get the folks on the nForum to okay it first - so, post a question on the nForum.
can we embed an iframe in a page?
I have no idea what that even means, sorry.
Try it. :upside_down:
The stuff you can make on Azimuth Wiki should be the same as on the nLab, right?
Approximately, but the nLab has been getting various upgrades.
ah, in that case, we won't be able to
we really just need to be able to host our own page
So for example if some insane person wants TikZ diagrams in the calendar they can do it on the nLab.

John Baez said:

So for example if some insane person wants TikZ diagrams in the calendar they can do it on the nLab.

So what @Nathanael Arkor is talking about is the possibility to display the calendar as embedded in the page, like what happens sometimes with youtube videos. For us to be able to do so we need some particular properties of the website, which nlab doesn't have. :frown:
We want the calendar embedded because it turns out this is the only way to be able to see all the multiple calendars we put in the calendar at once
I suppose there is an alternative, which is just to list all the calendars separately
though I think having the combined calendar is much more helpful
but maybe that's the best starting point
Anyone interested in seeing our analytics?

OK, we now have http://categorytheory.world/ to work with. If not useful I'm only out $2. I thought the .world suffix was appropriate for Azimuth, plus the .com domain may not be available. I'm a systems administrator, web developer and other useful stuff. I can look at calendars for example. I have root access on Siteground, so I have all kinds of resources.

When I click on http://categorytheory.world/ I get a stern warning from Firefox:

Warning: Potential Security Risk Ahead

Firefox detected a potential security threat and did not continue to categorytheory.world. If you visit this site, attackers could try to steal information like your passwords, emails, or credit card details.

What can you do about it?

The issue is most likely with the website, and there is nothing you can do to resolve it. You can notify the website’s administrator about the problem.
@John Baez I'm working on the problem. I need to install a certificate. Also the software I'm using doesn't like the .world suffix. Should be straighten out today. Thanks for your interest.
@John Baez @Christian Williams OK, https://categorytheory.world/ is running with the events calendar. Let me know what else might be nice to put up. I saw there were issues with multiple calendars.

:tada: Yay, great!!! :tada:

Thanks! Soon I will announce this to the world.

It would be very nice to add the ACT@UCR seminar. The calendar for that is here:

https://sites.google.com/ucr.edu/actucr/

It's at 17:00 UTC on Wednesdays, and the known talks are these:

April 8: Prakash Panangaden

April 15: Jules Hedges

April 22: Michael Shulman

April 29: Gershom Bazerman

May 6: Sarah Rovner-Frydman

May 13: Tai-Danae Bradley

May 20: Gordon Plotkin

June 3: Nina Otter
@Christian Williams is/should be working on that
Or I can PM the admin link to someone else

Thank you @Daniel Geisler and others for setting this up!

If you're looking for the iCal feed to integrate with Google Calendar or whatever you use, follow these instructions: https://calendar.teamup.com/kb/subscribe-to-teamup-icalendar-feeds/
Or just copy this: https://ics.teamup.com/feed/kswejyg8r137xttzjt/0.ics
:grinning_face_with_smiling_eyes:
Great job! Thanks!
OK, https://categorytheory.world/ has two calendars and other info. Check it out and give me feedback for what I need to change. I suspect we would do better with one calendar. What do folks think?

One seems best.

I was hoping the ACT@UCR information would be put into the same calendar as the rest.... maybe the idea is that Christian would do that?
OK, I'll get on it. There is an issue of duplicate entries between the calendars.
we already have one Google calendar with everything
@Christian Williams it is customary for the webmaster to have their email address on the front page of a site.
I think it would make sense to have the calendar start on Monday, which is the start of the working week, as talks are unlikely to be on Saturday or Sunday
